question.text: Which of the following is NOT a member of the Advisory Committee constituted for delisting under SEBI Delisting Regulations, 2021?

answer.text: A representative of an investor association recognized by SEBI
A representative of the Reserve Bank of India
An Executive Director or Secretary of the stock exchange

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Advisory Committee for delisting comprises: (a) one CA, (b) one representative of a SEBI-recognized investor association, (c) one representative of MCA/RoC, and (d) an Executive Director/Secretary of the stock exchange. RBI is not part of this committee. **The Trap:** Since RBI is a major financial regulator, candidates may assume it has representation on all SEBI-related committees, but the delisting advisory panel is specifically focused on investor protection and corporate compliance — not monetary policy. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) tests knowledge of SEBI committee structures in a different context. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding committee composition helps merchant bankers know which stakeholders to coordinate with during a delisting process.
